raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "The California State Assembly passed Assembly Bill 33 to ensure public safety and workforce protections as autonomous vehicle technology expands.",
    "AB 33 requires a qualified human safety operator onboard autonomous commercial delivery vehicles.",
    "Human safety operators will monitor autonomous vehicles for malfunctions and can intervene in emergencies while on public roads.",
    "AB 33 includes a framework for data collection by the Department of Motor Vehicles regarding autonomous vehicle operations.",
    "A legislative review process is included in AB 33 to evaluate and recommend safety impacts before permitting fully autonomous commercial deliveries without human oversight.",
    "There has been growing public concern over incidents involving driverless vehicles in San Francisco.",
    "A coalition of labor unions and consumer advocates supports AB 33.",
    "Opponents of AB 33 argue that it will stifle innovation in autonomous vehicle technology.",
    "AB 33 does not ban or delay the deployment of autonomous vehicles but provides regulatory guardrails as the technology develops.",
    "Nearly 80 percent of California voters support AB 33, emphasizing public safety and accountability as autonomous technology evolves."
  ]
}
```
